description: Professor Peter Bryant is Chief Executive Officer of the World Nuclear Transport Institute ( WNTI ), a not-for-profit that represents the global industry on the safe, secure and sustainable transport of radioactive materials. He has extensive experience in radiation protection and nuclear safety, having served as Director of ESG & Radiation Strategy and previously Head of Environment, Decommissioning & Radiation Safety at Sizewell C / EDF. In these roles he led Environmental Permitting, Funded Decommissioning, Radiation Protection, Radioactive Waste Management and Emergency Planning. Professor Bryant is a member of the International Commission on Radiological Protection ( ICRP ) Committee 4 and a recognised authority on radiation protection science. He holds an Honorary Professorship at the University of Liverpool, where he contributes to research and teaching in radiation protection and nuclear policy. He is also well known for his expertise in stakeholder engagement and risk communication, supporting the IAEA in international missions and teaching on its stakeholder engagement school. Appointed: October 2025 Term ends: September 2029

details.body:
Professor Peter Bryant is Chief Executive Officer of the World Nuclear Transport Institute (WNTI), a not-for-profit that represents the global industry on the safe, secure and sustainable transport of radioactive materials.

He has extensive experience in radiation protection and nuclear safety, having served as Director of ESG & Radiation Strategy and previously Head of Environment, Decommissioning & Radiation Safety at Sizewell C / EDF. In these roles he led Environmental Permitting, Funded Decommissioning, Radiation Protection, Radioactive Waste Management and Emergency Planning.

Professor Bryant is a member of the International Commission on Radiological Protection (ICRP) Committee 4 and a recognised authority on radiation protection science. He holds an Honorary Professorship at the University of Liverpool, where he contributes to research and teaching in radiation protection and nuclear policy.

He is also well known for his expertise in stakeholder engagement and risk communication, supporting the IAEA in international missions and teaching on its stakeholder engagement school.

Appointed: October 2025
Term ends: September 2029

In these roles he led Environmental Permitting, Funded Decommissioning, Radiation Protection, Radioactive Waste Management and Emergency Planning.

Professor Bryant is a member of the International Commission on Radiological Protection (ICRP) Committee 4 and a recognised authority on radiation protection science. He holds an Honorary Professorship at the University of Liverpool, where he contributes to research and teaching in radiation protection and nuclear policy.

He is also well known for his expertise in stakeholder engagement and risk communication, supporting the IAEA in international missions and teaching on its stakeholder engagement school.

Term ends: September 2029

title: Professor Peter Bryant
